headline: Berkshire’s US$10b Alphabet Bet Opens New AI Chapter Under Abel
summary: Berkshire Hathaway, NYSE:BRK.B, has committed $10 billion in a private placement in Alphabet focused on artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion. The investment is described as Berkshire's first major AI exposure under new CEO Greg Abel. This move marks a clear shift in capital allocation toward a leading technology platform. Berkshire Hathaway, NYSE:BRK.B, is stepping into AI at scale while its stock trades around $488.13. Over the past 3 years the stock is up 45.6% and over 5...

headline: How durable is the impact of the World Cup on stocks?
summary: Investing.com -- The 2026 FIFA World Cup, the largest in the tournament’s history with 48 teams, 104 matches and 16 host cities across the United States, Canada and Mexico, is expected to generate measurable but largely short-lived gains for a select group of U.S. companies, while media engagement may provide a more durable revenue opportunity than in-person attendance, according to analysts at Barclays.

headline: Alphabet Weighs SpaceX AI Deal And $85b Raise Against Valuation Concerns
summary: Alphabet (NasdaqGS:GOOGL) has signed a multi year AI infrastructure agreement with SpaceX to access more than 100,000 Nvidia GPUs housed in SpaceX data centers. The deal is tied to Alphabet's record US$85b equity raise, anchored by Berkshire Hathaway, to fund expanded AI compute capacity. The arrangement highlights growing competition for high end chips between large AI players and points to SpaceX's data centers playing a larger role in AI supply chains. Alphabet enters this agreement with...

headline: WWDC 2026 Puts Apple’s AI Pivot And Services Valuation In Focus
summary: Apple plans to use WWDC 2026 to outline a broad AI plan, centered on a relaunch of Siri powered by Google's Gemini models and Nvidia hardware. The company is expected to introduce a paid Siri subscription and extend its Apple Intelligence platform across more devices and services. This move points to a clearer focus on AI driven services revenue and deeper integration of third party AI technology within the Apple ecosystem. headline: Why UBS remains bullish on the hyperscaler stocks despite capex concerns
summary: Investing.com -- UBS maintained "buy" ratings on three major cloud providers as their combined revenue rose to $84.8 billion in the first quarter of 2026, up 39% from a year earlier, marking a 15-percentage-point acceleration from the growth rate recorded in the same period last year.
